text: Moving from one space to another, I feel the cool detachment of modernity. The broad, quiet room, with its beanbags and muted tones, suggests a place meant for reflection, yet it feels strangely sterile and impersonal—an architecture of purpose without the warmth of presence. The transition into the R+D area, with its vibrant, informative display, brings a rush of curiosity. The bold letters and the promise of evolving research stir my sense of wonder, yet I’m reminded that even innovation is curated and controlled. In this environment, I am both participant and observer, aware of my place in an ongoing dialogue between knowledge and experience. There’s comfort in the order but restlessness in the void of meaning still waiting to be filled.
